description: "This animated doll is about two feet tall, with porcelain skin that is chipped and cracked in places. Its round, glassy eyes are wide open, staring unblinkingly as it moves with jerky, unnatural motions. Dressed in a tattered, old-fashioned dress, it clutches a small wooden toy or a child’s accessory in one hand. Its once-rosy cheeks are now pale, and a faint grin is permanently etched into its painted face, making its expression both innocent and menacing at the same time. \r\n\r\nThese dolls were originally crafted as gifts for children by an eccentric toy maker who poured his heart and soul into each creation. However, when his beloved daughter tragically passed away, the grief-stricken toy maker turned to dark magic, binding malevolent spirits to his once-wholesome creations. Now, these animated dolls roam freely, filled with twisted, childlike malice. They often remain motionless, pretending to be harmless toys until the moment is right to strike, their eternal mission to seek vengeance on those who wronged their creator."

[br]\r\n[b]Magic Resistance:[/b] The animated doll has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects. [br]\r\n[b]Keen Hearing:[/b] The doll has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing. [br]\r\n[b]Multiattack:[/b] The animated doll makes two slam attacks. "

[br]\r\n[b]Hit:[/b] 6 [roll:1d6+2] bludgeoning damage. \r\n\r\n[h3]Creepy Lullaby[/h3]\r\n[b]Recharge 5–6[/b][br]\r\nThe doll lets out a haunting melody, forcing all creatures within 20 feet that can hear it to make a DC 12 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened for 1 minute. The frightened target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. "

Standing taller, this construct's form is bulkier and covered in reinforced armor plating with a dark bronze finish. \r\nIts eyes glow a fierce crimson, and it radiates a palpable aura of menace. \r\nGears and mechanisms, even more intricate than those of the sentinel, are faintly visible, showcasing the advanced craftsmanship behind this mechanical behemoth. \r\nTwo blade arms give it a more menacing appearance, and its movements are swift, surprising for its size. \r\nA faint humming sound emanates from its core, and occasionally it releases jets of steam from vents in its armor, indicating the immense power contained within. \r\nThis guardian is not just designed to deter intruders but to utterly annihilate any threat to its charge."

description: "The Clockwork Shadow is an eerie fusion of mechanical precision and shadowy substance, its form appearing as if woven from tendrils of darkness bound together by intricate gears and cogs. Its skeletal, humanoid silhouette is unsettlingly fluid, enabling it to contort and maneuver through the tightest of spaces. Luminescent glyphs glow faintly from within its chest, casting otherworldly reflections onto its complex internal mechanisms. \r\n\r\nDespite its mechanical nature, the Clockwork Shadow moves with a sinuous grace, as if gliding on a layer of darkness. Silent as a whisper and nearly as elusive as an actual shadow, it seems almost as though the Clockwork Tower itself birthed this construct to act as its spectral guardian. With shadowy blades that materialize from its hands and the uncanny ability to phase through solid objects, this construct is not only a marvel of artificery but also a deadly opponent."

Built with reinforced metals and imbued with advanced mechanisms, it is both a marvel of engineering and an imposing figure of defense. With an extended reach and enhanced functions, this guardian not only performs repairs but also acts as the frontline of protection against threats. \r\n\r\nIts central core emits a dim glow, signifying its heightened energy source. Its primary directive is the protection of the library and its denizens, making it a formidable foe for any would-be intruder. In its presence, the smaller Cogsprocket Spiders work with increased coordination, their combined efforts a testament to the Clockwork Tower's genius engineering."

These mechanical arachnids, each the size of a large housecat, skitter through the halls and across the walls and ceilings of the tower, tending to the machinery with deft precision. Each spider is a wonder of miniature engineering, with a gleaming brass body and eight slender, articulated legs, each tipped with an array of delicate tools. Their crystalline eyes flicker with an inner light, scanning their surroundings for any signs of disrepair. While generally non-aggressive, they will defend the tower if provoked. In numbers, they become a notable threat, attacking intruders with their sharp mandibles and nimble, swarm-like coordination. Always working, always mending, the Cogsprocket Spiders are an ever-present aspect of the tower''s unique ecosystem.'

Its body is a collection of intricate gears, arms, and tools, all meant to craft and repair its smaller Cogsprocket Spider counterparts quickly. While not as combative as the Cogsprocket Sentinel, it plays a vital role in maintaining the swarm's numbers during confrontations.\r\n\r\nThe Weaver has a set of retractable tendrils that it uses for both crafting and defense. Its underbelly houses a mini-factory, capable of churning out the smaller spiders with impressive speed. This mini-factory glows  with a soft blue light, indicating its continuous operation. When not in combat, the Weaver works tirelessly, producing, repairing, and modifying constructs, ensuring the Clockwork Tower remains in impeccable condition. If threatened, the Weaver prioritizes the creation and repair of its spider allies, leveraging numbers and maintaining operational integrity above all else."

description: "The cursed doll is a grotesque, human-sized figure crafted from porcelain and fabric, with a cracked and weathered face. Its once-beautiful features are marred by deep fractures, and its eyes glow faintly with a sickly green light. The doll wears an elegant but tattered dress, its fabric frayed and stained with dark patches. Its long, curly hair is matted and tangled, and its stiff limbs move with unnatural precision, jerking in sharp, sudden motions. There’s an eerie grace to its movements, as if it’s being puppeteered by some unseen force. \r\n\r\nOnce a cherished childhood companion, the cursed doll became a vessel for a malevolent spirit after its owner—a girl wronged in life—died under tragic circumstances. Bound by grief and twisted by dark magic, the doll now carries the vengeful spirit’s desire for retribution. The curse that binds it has allowed the doll to grow in power, turning it into a sinister force capable of manipulating other toys to do its bidding. Its charm is as deadly as its necrotic touch, luring victims into a false sense of safety before draining their very life essence. Many believe that the doll still seeks its lost owner, forever wandering in search of someone to control."

The charmed target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. \r\n[h3]Animate Toys[/h3][b]Recharge 5–6[/b][br]\r\nAs a bonus action, the cursed doll animates 1d4 nearby toys, which become hostile constructs under its control. description: "Standing over ten feet tall, its body is a complex amalgamation of brass, steel, and crystal, powered by an ever-shifting core of temporal energy. In one hand, it carries a large staff, pulsating with the same energy. \r\n\r\nOne of the most formidable inhabitants of the Clockwork Tower, the Eternity Warden is a mechanical marvel. The Eternity Warden was one of Constantine's earliest and most successful creations, designed to safeguard the Timepiece of Eternity from any threats. Its purpose ingrained into its very being, it tirelessly patrols the tower, ready to utilize its timewarping abilities at the first sign of intrusion. With the ability to slow down time, teleport across short distances, and withstand significant damage, the Eternity Warden is a formidable foe for any who dare threaten the integrity of the Clockwork Tower.\r\n"

actions: "[h3]Multiattack[/h3] The Eternity Warden makes two slam attacks.\r\n[h3]Slam[/h3]\r\n[b]Melee Weapon Attack:[/b] +11 to hit [roll:1d20+22], reach 10 ft., one target.[br]\r\n[b]Hit:[/b] 19 [roll:3d8 + 6] bludgeoning damage.\r\n[h3]Temporal Ray (Recharge 5-6)[/h3] The Eternity Warden unleashes a beam of condensed temporal energy from its staff in [u]a line 60 feet long and 5 feet wide[/u]. Each creature in the line must make a [u]DC 18 Dexterity saving throw[/u]. On a failed save, a creature takes 33 [roll:6d10] force damage and is slowed (as the Slow spell) until the end of the Eternity Warden's next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half the damage and isn't slowed.\r\n[h3]Temporal Shift (1/Day)[/h3] The Eternity Warden can teleport, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space it can see."

description: "With its sharp, shining, metallic body and kinetic energy, the Gearhound is an awe-inspiring sight to behold. This clockwork canine, a construct of interlocking gears and polished metal plates, radiates a sort of industrial beauty. Its eyes, glowing with an eerie, soft light, reflect a keen intelligence that belies its mechanical nature. Its mouth, filled with razor-sharp metal teeth, reveals the construct's deadly purpose. Its finely crafted spring-loaded legs allow it to move at breathtaking speeds, bouncing off walls and ceilings with an uncanny agility. \r\n\r\nThe whir of its internal gears and the soft hum of its mechanisms are the only sounds that betray its approach. \r\n\r\nThese constructs, the loyal mechanical watchdogs of Haven’s Ironworks, are as dangerous as they are fiercely loyal. They patrol the perimeters of the Ironworks with a relentless dedication, a testament to the genius of their creator many human generations ago."

special_abilities: "[b]Ethereal Sight.[/b] The ghost can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when it is on the Material Plane, and vice versa.\r\n\r\n[b]Incorporeal Movement.[/b] The ghost can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 [roll:1d10] force damage if it ends its turn inside an object."
actions: "[h2]Withering Touch[/h2] \r\n[b]Melee Weapon Attack:[/b] +5 to hit [roll:1d20+5], reach 5 ft., one target. \r\n[b]Hit:[/b] 17 [roll:4d6 + 3] necrotic damage.\r\n\r\n[h2]Etherealness[/h2]\r\nThe ghost enters the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane, or vice versa. It is visible on the Material Plane while it is in the Border Ethereal, and vice versa, yet it can't affect or be affected by anything on the other plane.\r\n\r\n[h2]Horrifying Visage[/h2]\r\nEach non-undead creature within 60 feet of the ghost that can see it must succeed on a [u]DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened for 1 minute[/u]. If the save [u]fails by 5 or more, the target also ages [roll:1d4×10] years.[/u] \r\n\r\nA frightened target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the frightened condition on itself on a success. If a target's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the target is immune to this ghost's Horrifying Visage for the next 24 hours. The aging effect can be reversed with a greater restoration spell, but only within 24 hours of it occurring.\r\n\r\n[h2]Possession[/h2]\r\n[b]Recharge:[/b] 6[br]\r\nOne humanoid that the ghost can see within 5 feet of it must succeed on a [u]DC 13 Charisma saving throw or be possessed[/u] by the ghost; the ghost then disappears, and the target is incapacitated and loses control of its body. The ghost now controls the body but doesn't deprive the target of awareness. \r\n\r\nThe ghost can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect, except ones that turn undead, and it retains its alignment, Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, and immunity to being charmed and frightened. It otherwise uses the possessed target's statistics, but doesn't gain access to the target's knowledge, class features, or proficiencies. \r\n\r\nThe possession lasts until the body drops to 0 hit points, the ghost ends it as a bonus action, or the ghost is turned or forced out by an effect like the dispel evil and good spell. \r\n\r\nWhen the possession ends, the ghost reappears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the body. The target is immune to this ghost's Possession for 24 hours after succeeding on the saving throw or after the possession ends."

special_abilities: "[h2]Ethereal Sight[/h2] The ghost can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when it is on the Material Plane, and vice\r\nversa. \r\n\r\n[h2]Incorporeal Movement[/h2] The ghost can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. It takes 5 [roll:1d10] force damage if it ends its turn inside an object."
actions: "[h2]Withering Touch[/h2]\r\n[b]Melee Weapon Attack:[/b] +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target.[br]\r\n[b]Hit:[/b] 17 (4d6 + 3) necrotic damage.\r\n\r\n[h2]Etherealness[/h2]\r\nThe ghost enters the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane, or vice versa. It is visible on the Material Plane while it is in the Border Ethereal, and vice versa, yet it can't affect or be affected by anything on the other plane.\r\n\r\n[h2]Horrifying Visage[/h2] Each non-undead creature within 60 feet of the ghost that can see it must succeed on a [i]DC 13 Wisdom saving throw[/i] or be frightened for 1 minute. [u]If the save fails by 5 or more[/u], the target also [u]ages 1d4 × 10 years[/u]. \r\n\r\nA frightened target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the frightened condition on itself on a success. If a target's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the target is immune to this ghost's Horrifying Visage for the next 24 hours. The aging effect can be reversed with a greater restoration spell, but only within 24 hours of it occurring.\r\n\r\n[h2]Possession[/h2]\r\n[b]Recharge:[/b] 6[br]\r\nOne humanoid that the ghost can see within 5 feet of it must succeed on a DC 13 Charisma saving throw or be possessed by the ghost; the ghost then disappears, and the target is incapacitated and loses control of its body. The ghost now controls the body but doesn't deprive the target of awareness. \r\n\r\nThe ghost can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect, except ones that turn undead, and it retains its alignment, Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, and immunity to being charmed and frightened. It otherwise uses the possessed target's statistics, but doesn't gain access to the target's knowledge, class features, or proficiencies. \r\n\r\nThe possession lasts until the body drops to 0 hit points, the ghost ends it as a bonus action, or the ghost is turned or forced out by an effect like the dispel evil and good spell. When the possession ends, the ghost reappears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the body. The target is immune to this ghost's Possession for 24 hours after succeeding on the saving throw or after the possession ends. "

description: "Born from raw temporal energy, the Paradox Phantom is an eerie and unsettling presence. Manifesting as an insubstantial clockwork figure that flickers like an old film reel, the Phantom constantly shifts in and out of time, appearing both in the past and the future simultaneously. This constant temporal flux makes it extremely difficult to land a solid hit on the Phantom. \r\n\r\nThose who fight it will find the Phantom can reach out with a spectral hand to touch its foes, leaving them with a painful echo of the past that persists even after the initial contact. Those touched by a Paradox Phantom often report feeling as though they are reliving their most painful memories, a debilitating effect that can turn the tide of a battle in the Phantom's favor. Truly, to fight a Paradox Phantom is to wage a war against time itself."

Trapped in a cycle of endless repairs, they continue to maintain the machinery they cared for in life. Manifesting as floating apparitions wielding ethereal tools, they are surrounded by an aura of rust and decay. Parts of their spectral forms flicker with the ghostly image of an apron and workman''s clothing, and their eyes burn with a bright, otherworldly light. Despite their unsettling appearance, they are not innately hostile, but they will fiercely defend the machinery they are committed to repair.'

description: "The stenbumling troll is a type of giant that is  distantly related to trolls. It is large and muscular and can often be found in mountainous regions. The stenbumling troll is a younger version of a bergatroll, a kind of stone giant. \r\n\r\nBoth stenbumling trolls and bergatrolls spend the majority of their lifetimes hibernating for extensive periods. They aren’t necessarily evil but tend to be brooding and ill-tempered, especially if they are roused prematurely from their hibernation. However, stenbumling trolls are also known to be easily distracted by beautiful items and charismatic humanoids. According to stories, they sometimes ally with wood elves or help children return safety to their village in exchange for shiny trinkets or a song."

Standing at [u]over 15 feet tall[/u], this construct is crafted from a blend of sturdy metals and delicate clockwork components. Its body pulses with the soft glow of [i]Earthheart Emeralds[/i], and ghostly gears are visible, phasing in and out of existence within its frame. As it moves, faint echoes of ticking, like a colossal clock, accompany its steps. \r\n\r\nThe most impressive feature is its capability to manipulate time in its vicinity, a feature embedded within its very mechanics. Some say looking upon the titan is like staring into the past and future simultaneously, a bridge between what was, what is, and what might yet be."

If it hits a victim it will lash for 1-6 hit points of damage. A natural. roll of 20, or a roll 2 or more greater than that required to hit, means that it has wrapped itself round a victim, pinning his arms to his sides, and will auto￾matically (no 'to hit' roll needed) constrict for 1-6 hit points of damage each round thereafter until the tentacle or its victim is dead.\r\n\r\nTentacle. Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 3 (1d6) damage. The other long tentacle has a hollow needle of bone, 6\" long, at its end. That tentacle will also flail a victim for 1-6 hit points of damage and will also wrap itself round a victim on the required roll (20 or 2 or more greater than normal). This tentacle does not constrict; instead, the melee round following the one in which the victim was trapped, the needle will be inserted into the victim's flesh and he will be paralysed instantly unless he makes a successful saving throw. Through the needle, the creature will then inject a saliva-like fluid into the victim, taking two melee rounds to inject a full dose if the victim is man-sized. (This takes but one round if the victim is smaller than man-sized, three or four rounds if larger than man-sized). When the necessary dose has been administered, the victim's internal organs will begin to soften and the creature will suck them out of the victim's body through the needle. A man will die after two melee rounds of this treatment (adjust this figure as above for creatures smaller or larger than man-sized). If the tentacle is 'killed' during the time in which the saliva is being injected, it will detach from the victim and cure disease must be administered within an hour or the victim will die. Once the dose of saliva has been administered, however, only more powerful curative magicwill save the victim from death within an hour even if the tentacle is detached immediately. The heal spell will be effective, as will regenerate followed by cure wounds"

blocks.title: Manticore
blocks.textualdata:
name: Manticore
challenge_rating: '3'
types: monstrosity
size: Large
sizer: NONE
languages: Common
alignment: 'Lawful Evil'
description: "A monster in every sense of the word, a manticore has a vaguely humanoid head, the body of a lion, and the wings of a dragon. A bristling mane stretches down the creature's back, and its long tail ends in a cluster of deadly spikes that can impale prey at impressive range.\r\n\r\n[b]Evil Predators.[/b] Manticores are fierce killers that hunt far and wide for prey. They work together to take down particularly large or dangerous creatures, sharing the meal once a kill is made. A manticore begins its attack with a volley of tail spikes, then lands and uses its claws and bite. When outdoors and outnumbered, it uses its wings to stay aloft, attacking from a distance until its spikes are depleted.\r\n\r\nA manticore isn't particularly bright, but it possesses a malevolent nature and the ability to converse. In the course of attacking, it denigrates its foes and offers to kill them swiftly if they beg for their lives. If a manti core sees an advantage to be gained by sparing a creature's life, it does so, asking for a tribute or sacrifice equal to its loss of food."

blocks.textualdata:
name: Mimic
challenge_rating: '2'
types: 'monstrosity (shapechanger)'
size: Medium
sizer: NONE
languages: n/a
alignment: neutral
description: 'Mimics are shapeshifting predators able to take on the form of inanimate objects to lure creatures to their doom. In dungeons, these cunning creatures most often take the form of doors and chests, having learned that such forms attract a steady stream of prey.  Mimics can alter their outward texture to resemble wood, stone, and other basic materials, and they have evolved to assume the appearance of objects that other creatures are likely to come into contact with. A mimic in its altered form is nearly unrecognizable until potential prey blunders into its reach, whereupon the monster sprouts pseudopods and attacks.'

Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn 't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.\r\n\r\n[b]Adhesive (Object Form Only).[/b] The mimic adheres to anything that touches it. A Huge or smaller creature adhered to the mimic is also grappled by it (escape DC 13). Ability checks made to escape this grapple have disadvantage. \r\n\r\n[b]False Appearance (Object Form Only).[/b] While the mimic remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary object.\r\n\r\n[b]Grappler.[/b] The mimic has advantage on attack rolls against any creature grappled by it."

alignment: 'Chaotic Neutral'
description: "Picture if you will a massive lobster with wings.  100' wings. Each. Of course that's only on older ones that have lived at least 100 years. Upon reaching maturity at the age to 2 they are about 6' long with 12' wingspan but their rate of growth slows greatly, to about 1' wingspan / 4\" length per year. Coloring tends to mottled green/red when younger to mostly red after about age 50. Similar to a normal lobster, they molt as they grow with a new, soft, chiton shell emerging as the old shell splits and falls away.\r\n\r\nInspired by [url:https://www.reddit.com/r/dndmemes/comments/13j53x2/its_the_roc_lobster/]this Reddit thread[/url] and the artwork I created using [url:https://creator.nightcafe.studio/]Night Cafe[/url]. "
